_comment: Source-of-truth for expected verifier verdicts on vendored corpus files
Why this exists: the bpf-selftests under selftests/progs/ use the in-source
(also under selftests/progs/) and sched_ext corpus (under selftests/sched_ext/)
do not — verdicts there are asserted by per-test C harnesses in the kernel's
tools/testing/selftests/{bpf,sched_ext}/ directory. We mirror that intent here.
Verdict-source precedence consulted by the runner
Files keyed by basename — basenames are unique across our corpus and the
manifest stays stable across directory moves.
Each entry MUST cite the kernel-side harness or in-source comment that
establishes the verdict. Files whose kernel-side failure happens past the
verifier (struct_ops map registration, GPL gating, sched_ext runtime exit)
should NOT appear here — delete them or leave as 'accept'.

bpf_dctcp.c.note: TCP CC happy path; kernel tools/testing/selftests/bpf/prog_tests/bpf_tcp_ca.c::test_dctcp asserts load+attach succeed.

bpf_cubic.c.note: TCP CC happy path; kernel tools/testing/selftests/bpf/prog_tests/bpf_tcp_ca.c::test_cubic asserts load+attach succeed.

bpf_dctcp_release.c.note: Kernel prog_tests/bpf_tcp_ca.c::test_dctcp_fallback expects load to succeed; the fallback semantic is asserted at runtime, not verify time.

minimal.bpf.c.note: Minimal sched_ext example; runner.c expects clean load + run.

maximal.bpf.c.note: Exercises full sched_ext_ops surface; runner.c expects load.

maybe_null.bpf.c.note: Properly null-checks task_struct args (paired with maybe_null_fail_*); runner.c::maybe_null asserts load succeeds.

create_dsq.bpf.c.note: scx_bpf_create_dsq usage in init op; runner.c::create_dsq asserts load.

prog_run.bpf.c.note: Demonstrates BPF_PROG_RUN against a struct_ops method; runner.c::prog_run asserts load.

init_enable_count.bpf.c.note: init/enable op counting; runner.c::init_enable_count asserts load.

hotplug.bpf.c.note: CPU hotplug ops exercise; runner.c::hotplug asserts load.

numa.bpf.c.note: NUMA-aware scheduling primitives; runner.c::numa asserts load.

dsp_local_on.bpf.c.note: scx_bpf_dsq_insert with SCX_DSQ_LOCAL_ON; runner.c::dsp_local_on asserts load.

select_cpu_dfl_nodispatch.bpf.c.note: select_cpu without dispatch; runner.c::select_cpu_dfl_nodispatch asserts load.

select_cpu_dispatch_bad_dsq.bpf.c.note: Bad DSQ id chosen at RUNTIME (not verify time); runner.c::select_cpu_dispatch_bad_dsq asserts SCX_EXIT_ERROR after run, load itself succeeds.

select_cpu_dispatch_dbl_dsp.bpf.c.note: Double-dispatch detected at RUNTIME; runner.c expects load to succeed and runtime exit error.

select_cpu_vtime.bpf.c.note: vtime dispatch from select_cpu; runner.c::select_cpu_vtime asserts load.

ddsp_bogus_dsq_fail.bpf.c.note: Despite '_fail' suffix, the bogus DSQ id (0xcafef00d) is detected at SCHEDULER RUNTIME. runner.c::ddsp_bogus_dsq_fail asserts SCX_EXIT_ERROR after run; load is expected to succeed.

ddsp_vtimelocal_fail.bpf.c.note: vtime dispatch to SCX_DSQ_LOCAL rejected at SCHEDULER RUNTIME; load succeeds. runner.c::ddsp_vtimelocal_fail asserts SCX_EXIT_ERROR.

enq_last_no_enq_fails.bpf.c.note: SCX_OPS_ENQ_LAST flag without an .enqueue op rejected at struct_ops registration (past verifier). runner.c::enq_last_no_enq_fails asserts ops registration fails; verifier-only verdict is accept.

enq_select_cpu_fails.bpf.c.note: Calls scx_bpf_select_cpu_dfl from .enqueue, but that kfunc is gated to .select_cpu context. Verifier-time kfunc-context check rejects. runner.c::enq_select_cpu_fails asserts load fails.

maybe_null_fail_dsp.bpf.c.note: Dereferences MAYBE_NULL task_struct *p arg of .dispatch without null-check. Kernel marks dispatch's p arg PTR_MAYBE_NULL; verifier rejects. runner.c::maybe_null asserts load fails.

maybe_null_fail_yld.bpf.c.note: Dereferences MAYBE_NULL task_struct *to arg of .yield without null-check. Same shape as _dsp variant. runner.c::maybe_null asserts load fails.

task_local_storage.c.note: W7.1: tp_btf-based bpf_task_storage_get exercise; kernel prog_tests/task_local_storage.c::test_sys_enter_exit asserts load+attach succeed.

local_storage.c.note: W7.1: LSM-based multi-storage (sk + task + inode) get/delete; kernel prog_tests/test_local_storage.c asserts load succeeds.

test_snprintf.c.note: W7.1: positive bpf_snprintf coverage (BPF_SNPRINTF macro + various fmt specifiers); kernel prog_tests/snprintf.c::test_snprintf_positive asserts load.

test_snprintf_single.c.note: W7.1: the static program is loadable (`const char fmt[10]` lives in rodata, zero-initialized = empty NUL-terminated string, valid empty format). Kernel prog_tests/snprintf.c::test_snprintf_negative drives load-rejection by mutating skel->rodata->fmt with bad format strings BEFORE __load — a userspace mutation our static analyzer cannot model. Reclassified to `accept` so we match the kernel's behavior on the unmutated program.

test_d_path.c.note: W7.1: positive bpf_d_path coverage from fentry/security_inode_getattr + fentry/filp_close; kernel prog_tests/d_path.c::test_d_path asserts load.

test_d_path_check_rdonly_mem.c.note: W7.1: bpf_d_path called with a rdonly-mem buf (from bpf_per_cpu_ptr on a __ksym). Kernel prog_tests/d_path.c asserts load FAILS — helpers cannot write into rdonly mem.

linked_list_fail.c.note: Negative tests for linked_list kfuncs (`bpf_list_push_*`, `_pop_*`, `_node_in`); kernel prog_tests/linked_list.c asserts load fails. All 36 progs are __failure-style by file naming.

test_spin_lock_fail.c.note: Negative bpf_spin_lock tests; kernel prog_tests/spin_lock.c::test_spin_lock_fail asserts load fails.

kfunc_call_fail.c.note: Negative kfunc-call tests; kernel prog_tests/kfunc_call.c asserts load fails.

test_bpf_nf_fail.c.note: Negative bpf_nf kfunc tests; kernel prog_tests/test_bpf_nf.c asserts load fails.

struct_ops_private_stack_fail.c.note: Negative struct_ops private-stack tests; kernel prog_tests/struct_ops_private_stack.c asserts load fails.

find_vma_fail1.c.note: Negative bpf_find_vma test; kernel prog_tests/find_vma.c asserts load fails.

find_vma_fail2.c.note: Negative bpf_find_vma test; kernel prog_tests/find_vma.c asserts load fails.

tracing_failure.c.note: Negative tracing-attach tests; kernel prog_tests/tracing_failure.c asserts attach fails (load may pass).

rbtree_btf_fail__add_wrong_type.c.note: Negative rbtree BTF test; kernel prog_tests/rbtree.c asserts load fails on wrong-type add.

rbtree_btf_fail__wrong_node_type.c.note: Negative rbtree BTF test; kernel prog_tests/rbtree.c asserts load fails on wrong rb_node type.

struct_ops_maybe_null_fail.c.note: Negative struct_ops MAYBE_NULL test; kernel prog_tests/test_struct_ops_maybe_null.c asserts load fails.

test_xdp_attach_fail.c.note: Negative xdp attach test; kernel prog_tests/xdp_attach.c asserts attach fails.

uptr_update_failure.c.note: Negative __uptr update test; kernel prog_tests/uptr.c asserts load fails.

cpumask_success.c.note: Positive cpumask kfunc coverage; kernel prog_tests/cpumask.c asserts load succeeds for all entry points.

dynptr_success.c.note: Positive dynptr coverage; kernel prog_tests/dynptr.c::test_dynptr_success asserts load succeeds.

task_kfunc_success.c.note: Positive task kfunc coverage; kernel prog_tests/task_kfunc.c asserts load succeeds.

cgrp_kfunc_success.c.note: Positive cgroup kfunc coverage; kernel prog_tests/cgrp_kfunc.c asserts load succeeds.

user_ringbuf_success.c.note: Positive user_ringbuf coverage; kernel prog_tests/user_ringbuf.c asserts load succeeds.

test_sk_lookup.c.note: Positive sk_lookup coverage; kernel prog_tests/sk_lookup.c calls test_sk_lookup__open_and_load and CHECKs non-NULL before exercising runtime.

exceptions.c.note: Positive bpf_throw / exceptions coverage; kernel prog_tests/exceptions.c::test_exceptions calls exceptions__load with ASSERT_OK before runtime checks (the negative siblings live in exceptions_fail.c).

map_kptr.c.note: Positive map kptr coverage; kernel prog_tests/map_kptr.c calls map_kptr__open_and_load with ASSERT_OK_PTR before exercising kptr ref/release.

test_attach_probe.c.note: Positive kprobe/uprobe attach coverage; kernel prog_tests/attach_probe.c asserts test_attach_probe__load and test_attach_probe_manual__open_and_load succeed (negative cases sit at attach time, not load).

test_check_mtu.c.note: Positive bpf_check_mtu coverage; kernel prog_tests/check_mtu.c calls test_check_mtu__open_and_load / __load with ASSERT_OK before runtime checks.

test_module_attach.c.note: Positive module-attach coverage; kernel prog_tests/module_attach.c calls test_module_attach__load with ASSERT_OK before exercising attach.

linked_list.c.note: Positive linked_list kfunc coverage (sibling of linked_list_fail.c); kernel prog_tests/linked_list.c calls linked_list__open_and_load with ASSERT_OK_PTR before exercising push/pop.

arena_atomics.c.note: Positive arena atomics coverage; kernel prog_tests/arena_atomics.c calls arena_atomics__open + __load with ASSERT_OK before runtime checks.

cgroup_tcp_skb.c.note: Positive cgroup-skb coverage; kernel prog_tests/cgroup_tcp_skb.c calls cgroup_tcp_skb__open_and_load with ASSERT_OK on the resulting skel before install_filters.

get_func_ip_test.c.note: Positive bpf_get_func_ip coverage; kernel prog_tests/get_func_ip_test.c asserts get_func_ip_test__load + __attach succeed before runtime checks.

fentry_test.c.note: Positive fentry coverage; kernel prog_tests/fentry_test.c asserts fentry_test_lskel__open_and_load succeeds before runtime checks.

fexit_bpf2bpf.c.note: Positive fexit-on-bpf2bpf coverage; kernel prog_tests/fexit_bpf2bpf.c::test_fexit_bpf2bpf_common asserts bpf_object__load succeeds before exercising attach + runtime.

fexit_test.c.note: Positive fexit coverage; kernel prog_tests/fexit_test.c asserts fexit_test_lskel__open_and_load succeeds before runtime checks.

local_kptr_stash.c.note: Positive local-kptr-stash coverage (sibling of local_kptr_stash_fail.c); kernel prog_tests/local_kptr_stash.c repeatedly calls local_kptr_stash__open_and_load with ASSERT_OK_PTR before exercising add/remove.

test_map_ops.c.note: Positive map-ops coverage; kernel prog_tests/map_ops.c asserts test_map_ops__load + __attach succeed before runtime checks.

atomics.c.note: Positive atomic-ops coverage; kernel prog_tests/atomics.c asserts atomics_lskel__open_and_load succeeds before runtime checks.

bpf_loop.c.note: Positive bpf_loop helper coverage; kernel prog_tests/bpf_loop.c asserts bpf_loop__open_and_load succeeds before runtime checks.

rbtree.c.note: Positive rbtree kfunc coverage (sibling of rbtree_btf_fail__*.c); kernel prog_tests/rbtree.c repeatedly calls rbtree__open_and_load with ASSERT_OK_PTR before exercising add/remove.

test_helper_restricted.c.note: Negative helper-restriction coverage; kernel prog_tests/helper_restricted.c iterates each prog with autoload-one and ASSERT_ERR(load_should_fail) — every prog is expected to fail load due to helper-context restrictions.
